headline: 93°F Monday — water young beds by Sunday
weather_notes: Monday reaches 93°F — first heat spike of the season — with zero rain forecast all week.
Today (76°F) and Sunday (82°F) are the last two cooler windows before the heat arrives.
No precipitation through at least Monday; zone 2 was last watered May 7.
Overnight lows of 48–54°F pose no frost risk, but warm nights will accelerate surface moisture loss.

suggested_tasks.title: Water c1, w1, w4 before Monday heat spike
suggested_tasks.description: Beans are mid-germination (day 6) and 93°F Monday with no rain could stall or kill germination — water today or Sunday.
